name: Formative Chatbot Synthetic Inputs Support Prototype Feedback Testing, Not Scorer Validation
statement: Watts et al. use synthetic student inputs in a formative scientific-argumentation chatbot workflow to test prompt versions and generated feedback, making the evidence relevant to lower-stakes prototype development but not to automated scorer validation or summative decisions.
population_or_context: Middle-school scientific argumentation chatbot prototype and feedback design.
time_horizon: Full text retrieved and extracted on 2026-06-02.
quantitative_note: No numeric scoring-validation result is extracted for this stakes-boundary finding.
caveats: Synthetic inputs are used as chatbot prompt-test material, not as a scored assessment-response corpus.
The source informs lower-stakes formative development more than scorer validation.
evaluation_method: Synthetic inputs tested multiple chatbot system prompts and feedback-generation behaviors.
limitations: Does not address grading, summative, selection, or accountability use.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: The finding paraphrases the use of synthetic inputs for formative chatbot prompt and feedback testing.
